skill.name: mcp-builder
skill.description: Guide for creating MCP servers in TypeScript or Python, including tool design, transport choices, testing, and evaluation workflows.
skill.summary: Build and evaluate Model Context Protocol servers with practical implementation guidance.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-severity hits, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, license text, and security terminology in reference guides. The real risk is moderate: included evaluation scripts can launch user-supplied MCP server commands, pass environment variables, call remote MCP endpoints, and write reports, which is legitimate for the skill but unsafe against untrusted servers.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied MCP Server Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The evaluation harness accepts a command and arguments for stdio transport and passes them into the MCP stdio client. This is expected for local MCP testing, but running untrusted commands can execute arbitrary code on the host.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command path is explicitly user supplied and is used to create stdio server parameters. The behavior is legitimate for an evaluator, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Remote MCP Endpoint And Header Support
medium_findings.description: The evaluation script supports SSE and HTTP MCP URLs with custom headers. This enables valid remote-server testing, but it can send requests and authorization headers to attacker-controlled endpoints if users provide untrusted URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Remote URL and header parameters are intentionally supported and passed into MCP clients. I did not find hardcoded exfiltration, but misuse can expose secrets to untrusted endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Forwarding To Tested Servers
medium_findings.description: The harness parses KEY=VALUE pairs from command-line arguments and forwards them to the tested stdio MCP server. This is useful for credentials during evaluation, but users must avoid passing sensitive values to untrusted server code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly parses environment variables and passes them to the spawned MCP process. The reference example includes token-style values, making the risk concrete but still user-controlled.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Hits In Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Many weak-crypto, secret, network, reconnaissance, and shell-pattern hits occur in Markdown implementation guides and reference examples. These are instructional examples, not hidden runtime behavior in the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are reference guides and example snippets. They still can influence generated code, so I classify the scanner hits as false positives for package compromise but useful for review.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to MCP documentation, SDK README files, local examples, or license metadata. I did not find evidence that the skill silently calls those URLs outside user-directed research or evaluation workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation resources or examples. No evidence found of hidden callback URLs or automatic exfiltration endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Capability In Evaluation Harness
dangerous_patterns.description: The harness is designed to start a local MCP server process for stdio evaluation. Users should run it only with trusted commands and reviewed server code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This pattern is directly present and expected for MCP stdio testing. The danger depends on which command the user supplies.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Handling Through Environment And Headers
dangerous_patterns.description: The evaluation workflow supports environment variables and HTTP headers for authentication. Users should avoid sending production credentials to untrusted MCP servers or endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code and guide both show credential-style inputs. I did not find hardcoded credential theft, but the handling pattern has clear misuse risk.

content.value_statement: Teams need MCP servers that expose external services clearly and safely. This skill guides planning, implementation, testing, and evaluation for TypeScript and Python MCP projects.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to plan MCP tools around real workflows and API coverage.
Provides TypeScript and Python implementation guidance for MCP servers.
Covers stdio, SSE, and streamable HTTP transport choices.
Includes guidance for input schemas, output schemas, annotations, and error messages.
Provides an evaluation harness and XML format for testing MCP tool quality.
Links to reference guides for best practices and language-specific patterns.
limitations: It is a guide and script set, not a complete generated MCP server.
The evaluation harness requires a configured Anthropic API key.
Users must supply and trust the MCP server command or remote endpoint being tested.
Reference examples may need updates for SDK changes after publication.

use_cases.title: Design a new integration server
use_cases.description: Plan tool names, schemas, transports, and API coverage before building a new MCP integration.

use_cases.title: Improve an existing MCP implementation
use_cases.description: Review tool descriptions, pagination, response formats, and annotations against MCP best practices.

use_cases.title: Create quality evaluations
use_cases.description: Build realistic read-only evaluation questions and run them against a local or remote MCP server.

prompt_templates.title: Plan my MCP server
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new service integration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to plan an MCP server for [service]. Identify core workflows, tools, input schemas, and transport choice.

prompt_templates.title: Create implementation structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate setup for TypeScript or Python.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to outline a [TypeScript/Python] MCP server structure for [service], including auth, pagination, errors, and response formats.

prompt_templates.title: Review my tool design
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of an existing MCP design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to review these MCP tools for naming, schemas, descriptions, annotations, and missing workflow coverage: [tool list].

prompt_templates.title: Build an evaluation suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to create 10 read-only evaluation questions for this MCP server. Include verification strategy and expected XML structure.

output_examples.input: Plan an MCP server for a project management API.
output: A tool inventory grouped by projects, tasks, comments, users, and search.
Recommended read-only and write tools with clear annotations.
Transport guidance for local development and remote deployment.

output_examples.input: Review my existing MCP tool list.
output: Schema changes that reduce ambiguity for agents.
Warnings for destructive tools that need clearer annotations.

output_examples.input: Create evaluations for a GitHub-style MCP server.
output: Expected answer format for each question.
Guidance for running the evaluation harness and reviewing results.

best_practices: Use clear action-oriented tool names with service-specific prefixes.
Prefer structured inputs, structured outputs, pagination, and actionable errors.
Run evaluations with least-privilege credentials and trusted MCP servers only.
anti_patterns: Do not expose broad tools that hide important parameters or side effects.
Do not run the evaluation harness against untrusted local commands.
Do not pass production tokens to unknown remote MCP endpoints.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you design, implement, and evaluate MCP servers for external services and APIs.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers TypeScript with the MCP SDK and Python with FastMCP patterns.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete server automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, reference patterns, and evaluation scripts that you apply to your service.

faq.question: Can I test remote MCP servers?
faq.answer: Yes. The included evaluation script supports SSE and HTTP endpoints with optional headers.

faq.question: What credentials are needed?
faq.answer: The evaluation harness needs an Anthropic API key and may need service tokens for the MCP server under test.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with any MCP server?
faq.answer: Use it only with trusted server commands and endpoints because evaluations can execute tools and pass credentials.
